119846.explanation: SGP Neder-Betuwe considers it important to have capable and knowledgeable people in local government and believes that quotas are not helpful.

119850.explanation: SGP focuses on what is possible. At the same time, not every application fits every location, which requires customization.

119851.explanation: SGP aims to look at what is possible and believes the municipality should facilitate such initiatives where possible. At the same time, SGP is cautious with financial resources, especially because many national subsidies exist.

119852.explanation: As a municipality, we must comply with legal obligations. At the same time, we must carefully consider what is wise in terms of organization and handle resident support carefully. Where possible, we should collaborate with neighboring municipalities.

119856.explanation: It is not the municipality's role to organize such events.

119859.explanation: SGP finds it important that people with disabilities can participate in society and wants to facilitate that where possible. At the same time, SGP believes companies must take responsibility in this as well.

119860.explanation: SGP wants to facilitate entrepreneurs where possible. This also applies to business residences, with a maximum of one business residence per company to ensure the rural area remains rural.

119861.explanation: SGP considers it important that people, especially children, with limited financial means can participate in society. This includes municipal contributions to music lessons and memberships in clubs.

119862.explanation: SGP believes that no public funds should be allocated to a local broadcaster.

119865.explanation: The municipality should be facilitative according to SGP, including toward associations. However, we should not keep nonviable sports clubs afloat with subsidies.

119867.explanation: Safety is very important. SGP considers this mainly a task for the police and believes there should be enough neighborhood officers available. When there are not enough officers, this should be compensated with enforcement officers (BOAs).

119868.explanation: Safety is very important to SGP. If camera surveillance helps with that, we should not neglect it.

119869.explanation: Everyone should feel safe, especially women. If a shelter for domestic violence victims ('stay-away-from-my-man house') is needed, the municipality should facilitate it.

119873.explanation: SGP believes the municipality should remain a rural community and that we should cherish our village identities. At the same time, the current housing shortage requires the municipality to remain reasonably flexible and evaluate the impact of each initiative on the municipality as a whole.

119874.explanation: It is important that people can participate because it provides structure and gives people a sense that they matter.

119876.explanation: A municipality's poverty policy should not make people too passive. There should be an incentive to earn a living independently. However, the municipality should regularly evaluate whether anyone is struggling. For now, according to SGP, increasing benefits is not necessary.

119878.explanation: The current scheme suffices. When there is no charging station nearby but there is a desire for one, it can be requested.

119879.explanation: Traffic safety is very important to SGP. This requires customization. For example, reducing speed around schools is sensible, but generic measures such as a speed limit throughout the entire built-up area are not desirable.

119881.explanation: The municipality must ensure appropriate and affordable youth care so that young people who need help receive it, but also in a way that the municipality can bear the costs.

119883.explanation: SGP is not in favor of abolishing the dog tax.

119885.explanation: SGP strongly values Sunday rest. In His wisdom, God gave humans a day of rest. This day is meant to honor God, serve Him, and care for one another. Sunday is the day of rest in the Christian tradition.

119887.explanation: The costs for waste disposal should be cost-covering and kept as low as possible.

119889.explanation: Initially, parents are responsible for paying for school swimming. At the same time, SGP finds it important that children whose parents cannot afford school swimming can receive a contribution from the municipality so that these children can also earn a diploma.

119891.explanation: Our aim is 30% social rental housing, but the current housing shortage requires the municipality to remain reasonably flexible and evaluate the impact of each initiative on the municipality as a whole.

119892.explanation: Tall residential buildings do not suit a rural municipality like Neder-Betuwe. At the same time, the current housing shortage requires the municipality to remain reasonably flexible and evaluate the impact of each initiative on the municipality as a whole.

119893.explanation: SGP is basically not in favor of room-by-room rental to prevent the subdivision of houses for labor migrants. It is allowed if the owner also lives in the property.

119895.explanation: SGP opposes merger with neighboring municipalities.

119896.explanation: SGP aims to create a new business park in Echteld on the north side of the A15 adjacent to the existing Medel business park. This business park is mainly intended for local SMEs and should ensure a proper transition from large companies to the surrounding rural area.

119898.explanation: SGP strongly values Sunday rest. In His wisdom, God gave humans a day of rest. This day is meant to honor God, serve Him, and care for one another. Sunday is the day of rest in the Christian tradition.

119846.explanation: Quality is the most important criterion.

119850.explanation: The sale of own products is already permitted.

119851.explanation: Within the rules of the Future-proof Living Regulation Gelderland, municipality of Neder-Betuwe, this is possible.

119852.explanation: The implementation of the distribution law must be carried out.

119856.explanation: The municipality facilitates initiatives for fireworks shows.

119859.explanation: It is the entrepreneur's own responsibility to arrange this.

119860.explanation: It is not intended that farmyards be fully built up. Current arrangements for stopping and demolition are sufficient.

119862.explanation: The current local broadcaster is too far removed from Neder-Betuwe. In other words, it covers too large an area to be truly local.

119865.explanation: It is important to identify the cause and take action accordingly.

119867.explanation: Visibility of the enforcement officers is essential.

119868.explanation: Proportional supervision is not a problem.

119873.explanation: The focus is on the centers and the edges of the centers.

119874.explanation: It can be useful if reintegration is an option.

119876.explanation: This is already well arranged. However, the existing arrangements need to be better publicized.

119878.explanation: The current working method does not justify changing this.

119879.explanation: It benefits the (traffic) safety of the entire village, not just the village center.

119881.explanation: Price can never be the sole criterion.

119885.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneurs to do this, but they should be given the opportunity.

119887.explanation: The current system is sufficient. Payment is required for construction and demolition waste.

119889.explanation: In this watery area it is important that everyone can swim.

119891.explanation: More than 30% is not a problem.

119892.explanation: Feasibility must be investigated per location.

119893.explanation: Encouraging housing division should be investigated and/or implemented.

119895.explanation: Currently there is no reason. Regional cooperation is important.

119896.explanation: The focus is on finding smaller business parks for local entrepreneurs and upgrading existing business parks.

119898.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneur to decide whether to open, not the municipality.

119846.explanation: Quality and suitability should be leading, not gender.

119850.explanation: Entrepreneurship in rural areas must be given space to keep the agricultural sector vital.

119851.explanation: We want to think positively and critically about climate measures and encourage initiatives from residents.

119852.explanation: We take our responsibility, provided it fits the scale of our villages (small-scale).

119856.explanation: This depends on the costs and the wishes of the residents; safety is paramount.

119859.explanation: Accessibility is important, but entrepreneurs also have their own responsibility.

119860.explanation: We must be careful with our rural area and prevent fragmentation.

119861.explanation: Sport is important, but there are already various arrangements (such as the Participate Fund).

119862.explanation: Local information provision is good, but must also encourage self-reliance.

119865.explanation: Associations are the backbone of the villages, but structural subsidies are not automatic.

119867.explanation: Safety is a priority; prevention and enforcement in the neighborhoods are essential.

119868.explanation: As a means to increase safety and tackle crime (which does not stop at the border).

119869.explanation: Care and safety for vulnerable residents must be well arranged locally or regionally.

119873.explanation: Filling in existing areas takes precedence over expansion, but sometimes building on the edges is necessary to provide enough housing.

119874.explanation: You get something in return; participation in society is good for everyone.

119876.explanation: Poverty reduction must focus on customized solutions and work, not just on income limits.

119878.explanation: We facilitate the transition to sustainable transportation.

119879.explanation: Traffic safety in residential areas is a priority.

119881.explanation: Quality of care for our youth is more important than just the lowest price.

119883.explanation: This tax is outdated and does not provide any direct reciprocity.

119885.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have the freedom to decide themselves whether to open.

119887.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping and makes it easier for residents to separate waste.

119889.explanation: Every child must be able to swim; this is part of basic education and safety.

119891.explanation: There is a great shortage of affordable housing for our own residents.

119892.explanation: We must build, but it must fit the village character of our communities.

119893.explanation: This can be a solution for housing shortages among young people or migrant workers, provided it is well regulated.

119895.explanation: Cooperation may be necessary to maintain administrative capacity and services.

119896.explanation: Good for local employment and the economy.

119898.explanation: We respect Sunday rest but do not impose it mandatorily on others.

119846.explanation: We want women and men to be well represented. The most important thing is that the best people with a heart for the municipality are in the executive board. We want to encourage more women to participate in politics but do not want to impose a strict obligation.

119850.explanation: We have great respect for farmers. Additional income through a shop or campsite can be good. Always allowing it goes too far. We must consider traffic safety, nature, livability, and fairness to other entrepreneurs. Therefore, a positive attitude with customized solutions per location.

119851.explanation: Residents who want to generate energy together show responsibility for creation and their environment. The municipality may support such initiatives, for example with knowledge, considering locations, and possibly subsidies. Support must fit within financial possibilities.

119852.explanation: A refugee center does not necessarily have to come to our municipality, but it can, provided it is carefully integrated. This means attention to safety, good guidance, and consultation with neighbors to take concerns and expectations seriously.

119856.explanation: A central fireworks show can bring people together and is often safer than a lot of fireworks in residential streets. It fits the idea of a strong community and celebrating together. The municipality must carefully weigh the costs and check if there is support among residents and associations.

119859.explanation: We want an inclusive municipality where people with disabilities can participate. Accessible shops and buildings are part of this. Entrepreneurs sometimes struggle financially, so the municipality may help, for example with advice, loans, or subsidies.

119860.explanation: We see farmers as partners in the landscape and understand their desire to build houses on their farms, for example for family or employees. At the same time, space is scarce and we must protect nature, water, and the landscape. Therefore, we want careful policies but no freedom without rules.

119861.explanation: Sports are important for health and social participation. Our program states that everyone should be able to participate, including people with low incomes. Therefore, we want the municipality to financially support membership of a (sports) club for residents receiving social assistance.

119862.explanation: Local media are important for information about villages, politics, and associations. They strengthen the community and give residents a voice. Funding can help improve quality and reach more people. The broadcaster must be independent and cooperate well with associations and the municipality.

119865.explanation: Sports clubs are the "glue of society." They provide meeting places, health benefits, and volunteer work. If a club struggles, the municipality wants to think along and, if possible, provide financial help, for example temporarily or linked to an improvement plan.

119867.explanation: Safety and livability are basic conditions for pleasant living. Enforcement officers (boa’s) can provide supervision, address behavior, and enforce rules. If the pressure on current boa’s increases, additional boa’s are justified provided the costs are reasonable and the deployment is clear.

119868.explanation: Camera surveillance can help prevent crime, especially in risk locations. At the same time, privacy and trust in public spaces are important. We want cameras only in problem areas, with good rules, clear evaluation, and as part of a broader safety approach.

119869.explanation: Violence, especially against women, is unacceptable. Victims must quickly and safely find help. A dedicated facility in or near the municipality can lower barriers. If a fully independent facility is financially or practically not feasible, we seek regional cooperation.

119873.explanation: We want sufficient housing for young and old. Sometimes building within the core is not enough. Then expansion beyond existing boundaries is needed, with attention to greenery, water, and accessibility. We build “in balance”: first within villages where possible, but not excluding expansion.

119874.explanation: We believe in both responsibility and solidarity. Those who can should contribute to society according to their ability. A reciprocity, such as volunteer work, can help people participate and establish a daily rhythm. This must be appropriate to a person's health and personal situation.

119876.explanation: More and more people with low or middle incomes have difficulty making ends meet. By raising the income limit, we can also help working poor and families with young children. We must pay close attention to the municipality’s financial capacity.

119878.explanation: Sustainable mobility belongs to a green and future-oriented municipality. More charging points make electric driving easier, also for residents without a private driveway and/or connection. We want to plan this well so that regular parking space in village centers is not overly pressured.

119879.explanation: Traffic safety, especially for children and cyclists, is important. More 30 km/h zones can reduce accidents and noise nuisance. Where many live, play, and cycle, and especially around schools, we clearly choose 30 km/h.

119881.explanation: We believe trust in care providers and quality and stability are important. If we focus mainly on price, we risk lower quality care and more turnover. Of course money matters, but in youth care quality and appropriate provision come first.

119883.explanation: We want to focus primarily on sufficient waste bins and good off-leash areas. Income generated from dog taxes should preferably be used for this.

119885.explanation: Rest on Sundays and space for family, church, and society are important in our program. We find it valuable that things do not operate 7 days a week. Therefore, we do not want shopping on Sundays. We also want to protect entrepreneurs and employees from pressure to always be open.

119887.explanation: We want people to properly dispose of waste and prevent illegal dumping. Lower barriers can help with this. At the same time, processing costs money and will affect waste collection charges.

119889.explanation: We live in a watery environment. Swimming skills are important for safety and health. The primary responsibility lies with the parents. In our municipality, parents can receive (financial) support through the Child Package so every child has the chance to learn to swim.

119891.explanation: Housing is a basic right. We want to build for starters, families, and people with lower incomes. A clear ambition for social rental housing helps to ensure affordability, especially now that waiting times are long. At the same time, we also want space for mid-range rent and owner-occupied homes.

119892.explanation: We want to respect the village scale and the landscape. Very tall apartment buildings do not fit in there. At the same time, building somewhat higher can help to build enough homes, for example along main roads. Therefore, we do not want extreme high-rise buildings, but moderate height where it can be well integrated.

119893.explanation: Room rental can help provide living space for employees or students. We support clear rules and tailored measures: allowing it in certain places with conditions (for example, that the owner must also be the main resident of the home), and limiting it in other places to protect livability.

119895.explanation: We stand for proximity: a municipality close to its residents. A merger increases the distance between residents and government. We want to collaborate with neighboring municipalities where useful, for example waste collection in regional partnerships, but remain functioning as an independent municipality.

119896.explanation: We are proud of our local entrepreneurs and family businesses. They provide employment and often contribute to associations and village life. If there is a need for more space, we support expansion with attention to accessibility, landscape, and sustainability.

119898.explanation: We find Sunday rest and space for church and family important. At the same time, Neder-Betuwe is diverse, and many residents want to play sports, meet, or have a drink on Sundays as well. We favor restraint and not a total closure. We seek a balance for each village and each facility.

119846.explanation: The qualities of the intended council members are the most important, not whether they are male or female.

119850.explanation: Completely agree. Entrepreneurs deserve our cooperation.

119851.explanation: The municipality must support where possible, but that does not always have to be with finances.

119852.explanation: As long as the distribution law is in effect, the municipality must comply with it. According to the VVD, we should do no more than that. We stick to the minimum necessary.

119856.explanation: A fireworks show in every village in cooperation with residents, entrepreneurs, and others.

119859.explanation: This is not a task for the government. Entrepreneurs are responsible for this themselves.

119860.explanation: Agreed, the municipality should take a facilitating role here.

119861.explanation: The municipality cannot solve everything. The costs must first be earned. We already have the youth sports fund for people who cannot pay for sports at the moment.

119862.explanation: The municipality must provide the funds intended for this. No extra resources; it must be earned again.

119865.explanation: The municipality must support sports clubs; this can be done in various ways.

119867.explanation: BOAs (public order officers) work excellently with the police and the App Group in our municipality. There is a need for more capacity.

119868.explanation: Camera surveillance is needed at hotspots such as the station in Kesteren and Opheusden and at places where waste is dumped.

119869.explanation: Fortunately, there is too little enthusiasm for this in Neder-Betuwe.

119873.explanation: The housing shortage is too great to impose extra requirements. There is a need for new homes for young people and the elderly.

119874.explanation: Everyone can contribute something to society. According to the VVD, we focus on what someone can do instead of what they cannot.

119876.explanation: Currently, the limit is 110%; the VVD wants to raise this to 120% as legally established. There are sufficient schemes available to use.

119878.explanation: At the moment, things are going well. Places are gradually increasing.

119879.explanation: It must be looked at per street. Reducing speed collectively does not increase traffic safety. It depends on the street design.

119881.explanation: It is also about the quality of care. Cheap is sometimes expensive.

119885.explanation: According to the VVD, responsibility and freedom lie with the entrepreneur. We respect everyone’s choice but also believe that the choice should lie with the entrepreneur themselves.

119887.explanation: Free of charge is not possible. The VVD adheres to the principle: the polluter pays.

119889.explanation: School swimming is extremely important, especially in a water-rich environment. Therefore, the VVD wants to allocate funds for school swimming.

119891.explanation: According to the VVD, we do not impose extra rules on housing construction, as that causes other homes to become more expensive.

119892.explanation: It is necessary to build upwards in Neder-Betuwe. The VVD has long been an advocate of this.

119893.explanation: We need to prevent subdivision of homes into multiple rooms.

119895.explanation: Neder-Betuwe is an independent municipality. According to the VVD, it should stay that way.

119896.explanation: Companies need space to expand. According to the VVD, we focus mainly on local SMEs here and not on dormitories full of labor migrants.

119898.explanation: We respect everyone’s beliefs. We believe that in Neder-Betuwe people should live together for and by everyone.

119846.explanation: Vóór Neder-Betuwe believes that quality, suitability, and support should be leading in forming the council. Equal representation is important but should not be a strict requirement.

119850.explanation: Vóór Neder-Betuwe supports secondary activities at agricultural companies but believes these must fit within the zoning plan, the environment, and livability. Automatic permission is therefore undesirable.

119851.explanation: Vóór Neder-Betuwe finds it important that sustainable initiatives are feasible but does not see financial support from the municipality as a given. Initiatives can be better supported via other schemes or collaborations.

119852.explanation: Vóór Neder-Betuwe believes that the reception of asylum seekers must be carefully considered and that the municipality's capacity should be leading. Therefore, a permanent asylum seekers' center in the municipality is not desirable.

119856.explanation: A fireworks show is possible but is not a fixed municipal task.

119859.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe values accessibility, but primarily sees this as the responsibility of the entrepreneur themselves. The role of the municipality lies mainly in regulations, raising awareness, and setting a good example in its own buildings.

119860.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe believes that housing construction in rural areas must be carefully considered. Building on agricultural land can be appropriate in some cases, but only with clear rules and tailored solutions per location.

119861.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe considers it important that everyone can participate in sports and physical activity. Support can be provided as is currently done, for example, via the child package, so participation is possible without new complicated arrangements.

119862.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe values a strong local broadcaster for information and involvement, but additional financial support should depend on necessity, performance, and clear agreements about the use of funds.

119865.explanation: Support may be necessary, but it depends on the circumstances and one's own efforts.

119867.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe finds safety important but sees more effect in good cooperation with the police, prevention, and sufficient neighborhood officers than in structurally expanding the number of enforcement officers (boa’s).

119868.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe considers camera surveillance a severe measure that should only be used in exceptional situations. Prevention, social control, and cooperation with the police are more effective and better for privacy.

119869.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe thinks it is important that victims of domestic violence are well supported. This support is currently regionally organized in Nijmegen, which is appropriate and effective. A dedicated facility in Neder-Betuwe is therefore unnecessary.

119873.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe believes housing construction must be carefully weighed. Infill within villages is preferred, but building outside village boundaries may sometimes be necessary to meet local housing needs, provided it is well integrated.

119874.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe supports an appropriate counter-performance if someone is physically and mentally able to do so. Socially useful work can contribute to structure, participation, and involvement in society.

119876.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe believes support should remain aimed at residents who need it most. The current income limits provide sufficient room for this, supplemented with tailored solutions where necessary.

119878.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe supports the transition to electric driving. More public charging points make electric driving more accessible and contribute to future-proof mobility.

119879.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe values traffic safety and livability in residential neighborhoods. A maximum speed of 30 km/h contributes to fewer accidents, less noise nuisance, and a safer street image.

119881.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe believes that quality, continuity, and appropriate care must be paramount in youth care. Price should never be the primary criterion if it compromises care for children and families.

119883.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe believes the revenue from the dog tax is needed for provisions such as placing and emptying dog waste bins and keeping public spaces clean.

119885.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe thinks the choice for shopping Sundays should fit local circumstances and support among entrepreneurs and residents. A general ban or mandatory allowance is therefore not desirable.

119887.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe believes household waste disposal at the recycling center should be free for private individuals. This encourages waste separation and prevents illegal dumping, while businesses are excluded from this.

119889.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe values school swimming but does not see it as a structural municipal task. Responsibility primarily lies with parents and schools, possibly supplemented by existing arrangements for families that need it.

119891.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe finds it important that sufficient affordable housing is available for residents with a lower or middle income. A minimum share of 30% social rental housing in new construction contributes to a balanced housing market and aligns with the national situation.

119892.explanation: High-rise buildings require tailored solutions per location, for example at De Hoge Wei, where the surroundings and scale are decisive.

119893.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe sees room-by-room rental as a way to better utilize the housing stock and create more living space, provided it is well regulated and does not cause nuisance.

119895.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe believes the municipality should remain independent. The current scale offers sufficient opportunities to serve residents well while preserving local identity and decision-making authority.

119896.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe considers expansion of the Medel industrial area important for employment and economic development, explicitly including space for smaller entrepreneurs so that local businesses can also grow.

119898.explanation: Voor Neder-Betuwe believes residents and entrepreneurs should be able to choose how they spend their Sunday. Mandatory closure does not suit a diverse and livable municipality.

statements.title: Neder-Betuwe should merge with a neighbouring municipality.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety should be employed in Neder-Betuwe.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Neder-Betuwe should expand the Medel business park.
Tall residential apartment blocks may be built in Neder-Betuwe.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: A women's shelter (a domestic violence refuge) should be established in Neder-Betuwe.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
There should be more CCTV surveillance in Neder-Betuwe.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Of the newly built homes in Neder-Betuwe, at least 30% should be social housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Neder-Betuwe moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Hospitality businesses, sports facilities and public services should be closed on Sundays.
